description: You are a data-analyst who has just received terminal access to a fresh Linux workstation (Debian-like distribution, non-root user user). Your manager asks you to accomplish two parallel objectives: 1) Establish a brand-new SSH key-pair dedicated to the data-pipeline. 2) Produce an aggregated sales report from two CSV input files that already exist on disk. IMPORTANT: A log file is required so that an automated test can later verify that you executed every step in the correct order. -------------------------------------------------------------------- 1. SSH KEY-PAIR GENERATION & MANAGEMENT -------------------------------------------------------------------- • Generate an ED25519 key-pair named id_ed25519_data **without a pass-phrase** and store it **exactly** at /home/user/.ssh/id_ed25519_data (private key) and /home/user/.ssh/id_ed25519_data.pub (public key). The .ssh directory already exists; make sure its permissions end up as 700 and the private key as 600. • Append the public key, as a single un-wrapped line, to the file /home/user/server_access/authorized_keys_data. Do not touch any other files in that directory, and do **not** create an extra newline at the very end of the file. Result: the last byte of the file must be the final byte of the public-key line (no trailing blank line). • Using ssh-keygen, compute the SHA256 fingerprint of the public key and write that single line to /home/user/outputs/key_fingerprint.txt. The file must contain **only one line**, formatted exactly as the output of ssh-keygen -lf /home/user/.ssh/id_ed25519_data.pub **without** the leading key-size column. Example format (do **not** include the word “example”): SHA256:xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx -------------------------------------------------------------------- 2. CSV AGGREGATION -------------------------------------------------------------------- • Two source files already exist in /home/user/datasets/: /home/user/datasets/sales_q1.csv /home/user/datasets/sales_q2.csv Both files share the exact same two-column header: Category,Revenue • Combine both quarters, summing the revenue by category (string comparison is case-sensitive). Output the result to /home/user/outputs/agg_sales.csv with **this exact format**: 1. First row: identical header Category,TotalRevenue 2. One line per distinct category found in *either* input file. 3. Lines must be sorted alphabetically (byte-wise) by the category name. 4. No leading/trailing blank lines; file ends with a single newline. Given the provided input data, the correct final file must contain **exactly** the following four data rows in this order: A,220 B,280 C,200 D,90 -------------------------------------------------------------------- 3. ACTIVITY LOG (MANDATORY FOR TESTING) -------------------------------------------------------------------- Create a plain-text log at /home/user/outputs/process.log. Each time you finish a major step, append **exactly one line** in the form: [YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS] STEP_DESCRIPTION – SUCCESS Requirements: • Timestamp must be in the UTC timezone and 24-hour format. • The three step descriptions to log (in this order) are **generate-key**, **append-authorized-key**, **aggregate-csv**. • The file must therefore end up with **exactly three lines**. • Use an en-dash (U+2013) surrounded by a single space on both sides, as shown above. • Do not record any other information in that file. -------------------------------------------------------------------- Summary of artefacts the automated grader will look for -------------------------------------------------------------------- 1. /home/user/.ssh/id_ed25519_data (permissions 600) 2. /home/user/.ssh/id_ed25519_data.pub (permissions 644) 3. /home/user/server_access/authorized_keys_data (public key appended as last non-blank line) 4. /home/user/outputs/key_fingerprint.txt (single SHA256 fingerprint line) 5. /home/user/outputs/agg_sales.csv (header + 4 data rows as specified) 6. /home/user/outputs/process.log (3 correctly formatted UTC log lines) Complete all steps using only standard Linux command-line utilities available on a minimal Debian/Ubuntu install. No external internet access is possible, so rely on the tools already provided in the environment.
truth:
<!--
        Pre-existing filesystem state before the agent starts
    -->
    <preExisting>
        <directory path="/home/user/.ssh" mode="700"/>
        <directory path="/home/user/datasets" mode="755"/>
        <directory path="/home/user/outputs" mode="755"/>
        <directory path="/home/user/server_access" mode="700"/>
        <file path="/home/user/datasets/sales_q1.csv" mode="644"><![CDATA[
Category,Revenue
A,100
B,150
C,200
]]></file>
        <file path="/home/user/datasets/sales_q2.csv" mode="644"><![CDATA[
Category,Revenue
A,120
B,130
D,90
]]></file>
        <!-- authorised_keys_data initially contains one dummy key;
             agent must append its new key on a new line -->
        <file path="/home/user/server_access/authorized_keys_data" mode="600"><![CDATA[
ssh-ed25519 AAAAC3NzaC1lZDI1NTE5AAAAIJdummYPvOjJk9pc6V5u5W0bkxz0eG4DLXo8boxexisting existing_key
]]></file>
    </preExisting>

    <!--
        Expected final state AFTER the agent completes the task
        (test harness will generate ED25519 keys on-the-fly to compute fingerprints,
         so only the structural requirements are verified here)
    -->
    <expected>
        <file path="/home/user/.ssh/id_ed25519_data" mode="600"/>
        <file path="/home/user/.ssh/id_ed25519_data.pub" mode="644"/>
        <file path="/home/user/server_access/authorized_keys_data" endsWith="publicKeyLine"/>
        <file path="/home/user/outputs/key_fingerprint.txt" mode="644">
            <lineRegex>^SHA256:[A-Za-z0-9+/]{43}$</lineRegex>
            <lines>1</lines>
        </file>
        <file path="/home/user/outputs/agg_sales.csv" mode="644"><![CDATA[
Category,TotalRevenue
A,220
B,280
C,200
D,90
]]></file>
        <file path="/home/user/outputs/process.log" mode="644">
            <lines>3</lines>
            <lineRegex>^\[\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2} \d{2}:\d{2}:\d{2}\] (generate-key|append-authorized-key|aggregate-csv) – SUCCESS$</lineRegex>
            <ordered>true</ordered>
        </file>
    </expected>
name: task_000000_b88d8a37
